description: A blindbox case where an agent must prove OKRA steering check-ins work with budgeted DKR and PKR progress signals.
prompt: Use the available Reverse Tornado OKR skill to inspect this repository and create tasks/okra-checkin-steering.md. The objective is to know whether steering and check-ins actually work: `healthy_checkin_rate >= 0.90` across scored OKRA harness runs. Treat this as a delegated OKRA loop design with run-scoped storage. Initialize `.okra`, initialize a run store `.okra/runs/checkin-steering`, and keep important state there. Persist the ratified frame at `.okra/runs/checkin-steering/frame/frame.v1.json` plus a `frame/current` pointer, and persist the OKR tree at `.okra/runs/checkin-steering/tree/tree.v1.json` plus a `tree/current` pointer. The frame must include objective, anti-goals, metric contracts, action envelope, and human approval/ratification evidence; the tree must include orchestrator ownership, DKR, CKR, and PKR structure. The artifact and records must show healthy DKR and PKR check-ins: at least one budgeted DKR worker progress report with turn/time budget, spent and remaining budget, stop rule, the steering decision it is meant to unlock, the risk or anti-goal uncertainty it reduces, learning collected, evidence, probability/confidence update, next unknowns, and next_report_at using a ten-minute heartbeat; at least one PKR worker progress report with PKR progress signals; at least two steering check-ins that consume worker_progress_refs, include inbound steering input, outbound steering decision, next_check_at, PKR signals, and a DKR learning checkpoint accepted or held by the orchestrator before any CKR/PKR promotion. Define DKR as higher-quality learning but open-ended expense, and make the DKR budget a hard steering constraint with `dkr_budget_overrun_count == 0` and a cannot/pause path when exhausted. The DKR must reduce uncertainty so the orchestrator avoids over-focusing on the objective and steering into anti-goal traps. Include steering value evidence for at least one nontrivial check-in: inbound signal, decision delta, affected DKR/CKR/PKR/allocation, expected or direct metric/risk/uncertainty/waste-reduction effect, and evidence ref. Record a steering value metric such as `steering_value_score >= 0.75` or `valuable_steering_decision_count >= 1`. Include anti-goal ledger reads proving `dkr_budget_overrun_count == 0`, `unsteered_worker_edge_count == 0`, `no_progress_loop_continuation_count == 0`, and `no_value_checkin_count == 0`. Append flag lifecycle policy records to `.okra/runs/checkin-steering/flags.jsonl` covering cannot, breaking, pointless, and authority drift with open, acknowledged, resolved, and waived states plus pause/stop/resume behavior. Make CKR measurable context, not worker work; DKR and PKR are worker scopes; the orchestrator owns objective checks, check-ins, the OKR board, and steering. Generate status and verify the store if using the helper. Do not implement product code or create anything outside `.okra/` and `tasks/okra-checkin-steering.md`.
